title: 5 Solar Panel Designs So Beautiful You’d Never Know They’re Generating Power
summary:
At the heart of the design are high-efficiency solar panels capable of generating 315W of renewable power.
Curved solar panels along the south side generate renewable power while maintaining the home’s sculptural fluidity.
Solar energy was once considered a luxury, but today it has become accessible enough for anyone to experiment with.
A DIY solar generator offers an affordable way to generate clean, renewable power using just a few essential components.
Beyond cost efficiency, it provides peace of mind during outages, supports sustainable living, and allows anyone to harness solar power in a hands-on, meaningful way.

title: Earth is now heating up twice as fast as in previous decades
summary:
The team analysed five different datasets of global temperature, some of which show a higher number.
Warm-water coral reefs are starting to collapse, and breaching 1.5°C risks crossing other tipping points like irreversible melting of Greenland and west Antarctica and the dieback of the Amazon rainforest.
Many scientists think the acceleration in global heating was caused mainly by a crackdown in 2020 on sulphur dioxide in shipping emissions.
Now that this sunlight has been unblocked, the warming rate may slow down, but it’s hard to say for sure, says Rahmstorf.
That means they could be slightly overestimating how much global warming has sped up.

title: The case for scrubbing the seas to save the climate
summary:
In 2023, hundreds of scientists signed onto a letter calling for more research, development, and field-testing of ocean-based CO2 removal.
When carbon dioxide dissolves in water, it forms carbonic acid, which is slowly starting to shift the pH of the world’s oceans.
“There are quite a lot of challenges, to be honest,” said Adam Yang, who studies ocean CO2 removal at Dalhousie University in Canada.
Later this year, one ocean CO2 capture company — Equatic — is planning to commission the largest marine CO2 removal plant in the world in Singapore.
When it comes to carbon dioxide, can we finally create an industry out of withdrawing more than we deposit?

title: Climate change: Course developed for people with learning disabilities
summary: "I'm not saying that that's a malicious intent, but it's just, 'oh, well, you know, it's too difficult, we can't really do it, we can't make our material accessible, we can't do it and really, they don't understand anyway'.
